PARC WP 5.3.1 Ab Initio

D.R. Schultz

03 July 2024

Pipeline for limma-voom & DRomics analysis of EUT046

Settings

Study information

  • Cell culture: RPTEC/TERT1
  • Compounds: Cisplatin Formulation preparation:
  • Cisplatin: cisplatin reconstituted in DMEM Dose levels:
  • Cisplatin: 0, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10, 20, 30, 50 uM
  • Time points: 0, 4, 8, 16, 24, 48, 72 hours Transcriptome analysis: at the specified timepoints, monolayers were lysed using lysis buffer and collected, samples were then snap frozen and send to BioClavis for TempO-seq analysis

Perform differential expression analysis using limma-voom

Load required libraries

pacman::p_load( BiocManager, limma, edgeR, biobroom, janitor, ggplot2, dplyr, tidyverse, clusterProfiler, broom, ggrepel, ggforce, RSQLite, Biobase, GO.db, pvclust, BiocParallel, pheatmap, RColorBrewer, reshape2, DOSE, purrr, tibble, skimr, doRNG, stringr )

Read in files:

  1. Raw count data from TempoSeq

  2. Manifest file with sample information

  3. Sample data relationship file

Countsx <- read.csv("EUT046_counts_per_gene_per_sample_raw_final.csv")
Manifest <- read.delim("Temposeq_manifest_Human_WT_1.2_realignment_96percent_2023-05-15.txt")
SDRF <- read.delim("Targets.txt", check.names=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

Calculate and visualise library sizes to check for poor quality samples

ggplot(librarySizeData, aes(x = Sample, y = LibrarySize)) +
  geom_jitter(aes (colour = Treatment, shape = Timepoint)) +
  xlab("Sample") +
  ylab("Library Size") +
  ggtitle("Library Size Distribution")+
  geom_hline(yintercept = threshold25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22))  # Specify shapes for each timepoint

Preprocess data (normalise (upper quartile), relevance filter, combine duplicate genes (based on EntrezID)

dge.norm <- calcNormFactors(dge,
                       method = "upperquartile",
                       p = 0.75)


cpm_values <- edgeR::cpm(dge.norm, normalized.lib.sizes = TRUE, log = FALSE)
min_replicates_threshold <- ceiling(0.75 * 3)
relevance_filtered_data <- cpm_values[rowSums(cpm_values >= 1) >= min_replicates_threshold, ]
relevance_filtered_data <- rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(relevance_filtered_data))

dim(relevance_filtered_data)
## [1] 14430   205
dge.norm.sum <- aggregate(entrez_data2[, -1], by = list(entrez_data2$geneid), sum)
dim(dge.norm.sum)
## [1] 13040   205

Check and apply pseudocount for DRomics analysis

df <- dge.norm.sum[,-1]
smallest_non_zero <- df %>%
  select_if(is.numeric) %>%  # Select only numeric columns
  unlist() %>%  # Convert to a vector
  .[. > 0] %>%  
  min()  # 
pseudocount <- smallest_non_zero * 0.1 # 10% of lowest
dge.norm.sum2 <- column_to_rownames(dge.norm.sum, "Group.1")
pseudo_count_data <- dge.norm.sum2 + pseudocount
dge.norm.sum.log <- log2(pseudo_count_data)

Visualise to check efficacy using MDS, PCA, correlations, densities, and boxplots

Continue with limma-voom with weights on separate time points

Perform Benchmark Dose Analysis using DRomics

Load libraries

pacman

Import data for benchmark dose analysis
